forecasts.reasoning: Beto O'Rourke is visibly touring Texas and teasing a 2026 Senate run, but an announcement before July 1, 2025, seems unlikely. In late April 2025, he explicitly told Texas Monthly it was "too early to make a definitive proclamation about 2026 or 2028." He has missed the canonical early-spring announcement window (having announced his 2018 Senate bid in March). Given only ~40 days remain until the July 1 resolution date, and mid-summer is historically a poor time to generate media momentum or robust initial fundraising compared to the fall, he is heavily incentivized to hold off. A late summer or autumn announcement (resembling his November 2021 launch for the 2022 Gubernatorial race) makes more sense to allow the Republican primary between Cornyn and Paxton to grow more divisive. The lack of exploratory FEC filings or definitive staff leaks as of late May further depresses the chances of an imminent announcement. While there's a small chance he launches in June to post a Q2 fundraising surprise, the preponderance of evidence (and his own words) points toward waiting past July 1.

A major military conflict ("Operation Sindoor") occurred in early May 2025 following a terrorist attack in April. India launched unprecedented kinetic strikes into Pakistan proper (Punjab, Islamabad). However, US President Trump brokered a ceasefire around May 10, which continues to hold as of May 21. PM Modi and Indian officials have stressed the ceasefire is merely a "pause" and demanded Pakistan hand over Hafiz Saeed, warning that future provocations will be met with immediate escalation. 

Despite the fiery rhetoric (which serves to maintain deterrence and manage domestic anger over an abrupt end to the war), the base rate of hostilities resuming the very next month without a new trigger is relatively low. Pakistan's military is on high alert and likely to restrain militant groups from launching major attacks across the border in the immediate short term to avoid a second round of strikes. Furthermore, breaking a US-brokered ceasefire in Pakistan proper (provinces of Punjab, Sindh, KP, Baluchistan) would invite major international diplomatic blowback. Thus, while tensions remain critically high, an attack specifically in June 2025 is somewhat unlikely unless there's an immediate, severe provocation.

The National UFO Reporting Center (NUFORC) primarily receives reports from North America. Pilot reports constitute a very small sub-category of all UFO sightings submitted to the center. While the background provides an example of one such sighting in Rwanda in January 2025, the occurrence of these specific conditions (a pilot report, occurring in Africa, reported to a US-based civilian UFO website) is exceedingly rare. Restricting this to a single specific month (June 2025) makes the base rate probabilistically minute. Given no specific events (like a planned Starlink deployment over Africa that might be mistaken for a UFO in June 2025) strongly suggesting an anomaly, I assign the lowest allowable probability to this event.

Rippling raised $450 million in Series G funding on May 9, 2025, alongside a $200 million tender offer, which directly aimed to provide liquidity while avoiding an IPO. CEO Parker Conrad explicitly stated that an IPO is "not imminent" and characterized Rippling as a company that does not want to go public yet. Given the recent substantial private funding, the launch of an alternative liquidity mechanism, explicit denials of IPO plans just weeks ago, and only a 5-week window remaining until the July 1, 2025 deadline, the probability of an IPO announcement is extremely low.

The government of Mali, currently led by a military junta under Gen. Assimi Goïta, issued a presidential decree on May 13, 2025, completely dissolving all political parties. This action was not an isolated decree but followed the formal repeal of the long-standing Political Parties Charter by the Malian legislature just days prior on May 12, 2025. This coordinated move demonstrates a strong structural intent to dismantle political opposition. Furthermore, the junta's recent "national conference" recommended that Gen. Goïta remain in power for a five-year renewable term starting in 2025, further illustrating a transition towards long-term authoritarian consolidation, mirroring the actions of allied military juntas in Niger and Burkina Faso. Given that the decree was enacted in mid-May and the resolution date is July 1, 2025 (only about 6 weeks later), there is practically no time or political will for the junta to suddenly reverse this landmark structural decision. While there is domestic pushback and international condemnation, the junta has historically ignored these pressures (e.g., pulling out of ECOWAS). A partial reversal (e.g., passing a new restrictive charter allowing new parties to form) within 6 weeks of formally abolishing the system is highly improbable. Therefore, the probability is assessed as 0.02.

With the knowledge cutoff at May 22, 2025, only ~39 days remain until the July 1, 2025 resolution date. Databricks drastically reduced its need for near-term public market capital and employee liquidity by raising a massive $10B funding round in December 2024, followed by a $5.25B credit facility in January 2025. CEO Ali Ghodsi stated multiple times in late 2024 and early 2025 (most recently on Bloomberg in Feb 2025) that there is "not an extreme sense of urgency" for an IPO, floating late 2025 or 2026 as potential timetables. There is currently no active reporting of underwriter selections or confidential drafts in Q2 2025. Given the lack of recent momentum and the recent massive private capital injections, an official S-1 filing or formal announcement of IPO plans by July 1, 2025 is exceedingly unlikely.

Background info indicates the question focuses on whether Trump or Musk will tweet the exact phrase "diversity visa". Searches have shown extensive recent discussion between Trump and Musk about H-1B visas and immigration in late 2024 and early 2025. However, there has been no record in news indexing of either of them tweeting the exact phrase "diversity visa" so far in 2025. While Trump has used the phrase in the past (e.g., 2017) and Musk follows an account (fentasyl) that recently tweeted about it, "diversity visa" is an exact phrase match requirement. Musk frequently replies to accounts with short affirmations ("!", "True") rather than repeating the phrase. Since we are already past the target date in the title (April 1) and very close to the one in the text (July 1), the likelihood they will proactively use this specific phrasing in the remaining weeks is fairly low.
